_comment: Field-Consensus Threshold (FCT) registry. Migrated in-place from shared/organ-weight-thresholds.json (species-magnitude-thresholds-dog-nhp Phase A, M1: no legacy versioning). OM content is byte-equivalent to the pre-migration JSON. Severity classification reads this registry as single source of truth for per-(domain, endpoint, species, direction, sex) native-scale bands. Phase B (upcoming) migrates LB/BW/classify_severity call sites.

_schema.entries: Keyed by '<DOMAIN>.<endpoint_or_test_code>.<direction>'. DOMAIN is the SEND 2-char code (OM, LB, BW, VS, CV, MI, etc.). direction ∈ {up, down, both} — 'both' when severity depends on magnitude of change irrespective of sign (e.g., OM liver hypertrophy OR atrophy can be adverse).
_schema.entries.<key>.species_specific: True when thresholds vary by species. When false, use bands['any'].
_schema.entries.<key>.bands: Per-species (or 'any' when not species_specific) band thresholds. variation_ceiling = noise ceiling (below = trivial), concern_floor = mild-effect floor (optional; null for OM — migrated 3-tier data has no concern tier), adverse_floor = biologically meaningful floor, strong_adverse_floor = clearly adverse regardless of context.
_schema.entries.<key>.bands.<species>.any_signif: When true, any statistically significant change is treated as adverse regardless of magnitude (rodent brain/testes pattern). Numeric bands may still be present as documentation.
_schema.entries.<key>.coverage: full = all in-scope species have non-null bands; partial = some species missing/null; none = no numeric bands (classifier emits 'provisional' verdict).
_schema.entries.<key>.provenance: Entry-level provenance is the LOWEST (most-extrapolated) species-band provenance — honest-uncertainty default. Individual band provenance, when present, overrides for species-resolved lookup.
_schema.entries.<key>.threshold_reliability: Summary tier: high = published regulatory + industry validation; moderate = industry survey or single-source regulatory; low = extrapolated across species; speculative = proxy / stopping criterion.
_schema.joint_rules: Multi-endpoint bundles (e.g., Hy's Law L01, CRS Grade-2). Each carries provenance and a syndrome_rule_ref that must resolve against the live syndrome engine at load time (bidirectional integrity — AC-F2-1).
_schema.cv_reference: Documentation only — published CVs justifying the threshold choices. Not consumed by the severity classifier.

_lb_hematology_sex_rationale: AC-F2-7: All LB hematology entries ship sex_specific: false. This reflects the SEND analysis convention of sex-matched control comparisons -- fold change normalizes out first-order baseline sex differences (e.g., male rats have higher baseline ALT than females, but male-treated vs male-control normalizes out baseline). CV-based band calibration (Bourges-Abella 2015 dog hematology) is pooled across sex; if pooled-source CVs mask sex-specific variance components, the band tightness may be marginally off for one sex. Tracked as RG-FCT-LB-BW-16; fold-change-over-sex-matched-control is the first-order mitigant. AC-F4-5 sex-balance column provides the empirical check.

source_refs.citation: JMPR/EFSA threshold of toxicological concern (historical practice)
Hall AJ et al. 2012. Liver hypertrophy: a review of adaptive (adverse and non-adverse) changes — conclusions from the 3rd International ESTP Expert Workshop. Toxicol Pathol 40(7):971-994.
Choi T et al. 2011. Beagle dog historical control organ-weight CV data.

OM.LIVER.both.notes: Liver is the most-studied organ for adaptive vs adverse distinction. Hall 2012 defined the LB panel gate. Dog thresholds wider due to higher CVs (Choi 2011: M=15%, F=21%).
OM.LIVER.both.dog_adaptive_notes: ALP elevation without bilirubin elevation should not preclude adaptive classification in dogs (C-ALP confound). ALT is the primary liver injury discriminator.

source_refs.citation: JMPR/EFSA threshold of toxicological concern (historical practice)
Choi T et al. 2011. Beagle CV data.

OM.KIDNEY.both.notes: Kidney weight changes >=10% are concerning. Male rat a2u-globulin nephropathy is not human-relevant. Dog uses single wider female-driven threshold (Choi 2011: M CV=12%, F CV=18%); 15% ceiling is conservative for males but defensible.

source_refs.citation: Choi T et al. 2011. Beagle heart CV data (M=10%, F=13%).

OM.HEART.both.notes: Heart has low normal variation (CV ~5-8% rodent). Dog heart CV is higher (Choi 2011: M=10%, F=13%).

source_refs.citation: Choi T et al. 2011. Dog brain CV ~5%.

OM.BRAIN.both.notes: Brain weight is tightly regulated. Rat/mouse: any statistically significant change is biologically meaningful (any_significant policy). Dog brain CV ~5% (Choi 2011), so dog uses 5% floor instead of any_significant.

source_refs.citation: Choi T et al. 2011. Dog adrenal CV ~20%.

OM.ADRENAL.both.notes: Adrenal weight is highly variable, especially in mice and dogs. Species-specific thresholds prevent false positives. Dog adrenal CV ~20% (Choi 2011).

source_refs.citation: Capen CC 1997. Mechanistic data and risk assessment of selected toxic endpoints of the thyroid gland. Toxicol Pathol 25(1):39-48.
Choi T et al. 2011. Dog thyroid CV ~15%.

OM.THYROID.both.notes: Thyroid weight changes often secondary to liver enzyme induction (liver-thyroid axis). Capen 1997. Dog thyroid CV ~15% (Choi 2011).

source_refs.citation: Choi T et al. 2011. Dog spleen CV ~20%.
Amato DV et al. 2022. Historical control data for nonhuman primate organ weights — spleen Tier C qualitative.

OM.SPLEEN.both.notes: Spleen weight is naturally variable. Large changes may indicate immune response or stress. Dog spleen CV ~20% (Choi 2011). NHP: Amato 2022 Tier C qualitative — no numeric FCT, classifier emits provisional verdict.

source_refs.citation: Choi T et al. 2011. Dog thymus CV ~25%.
Amato DV et al. 2022. NHP thymus Tier C qualitative.

OM.THYMUS.both.notes: Thymus weight is age-sensitive and stress-responsive. Involution is normal in older animals. Dog thymus CV ~25% (Choi 2011). NHP: qualitative only.

source_refs.citation: Choi T et al. 2011. Dog testes CV ~15%.

OM.TESTES.both.notes: Testes weight is tightly regulated in rodents. Dog testes CV ~15% (Choi 2011). Changes >=15% are concerning for reproductive toxicity. NHP entry absent pending species calibration (GAP-SMT-07).

source_refs.citation: Choi T et al. 2011. Dog epididymis CV ~12%.

OM.EPIDIDYMIDES.both.notes: Epididymal weight changes often parallel testicular effects. Dog epididymis CV ~12% (Choi 2011).

source_refs.citation: Historical ovarian weight CV surveys (estrous cycle stage variability).

OM.OVARIES.both.notes: Ovarian weight is highly variable due to estrous cycle stage. Higher thresholds prevent false positives. No dog-specific threshold -- ANCOVA-only for dogs (CV >60%).

source_refs.citation: Historical uterine weight CV surveys (most variable organ weight).

OM.UTERUS.both.notes: Uterine weight is the most variable organ weight due to estrous cycle. Very high thresholds required. No dog-specific threshold -- ANCOVA-only for dogs (CV >60%).

source_refs.citation: Amato DV et al. 2022. NHP lungs Tier C qualitative (perfusion/inflation variability).

OM.LUNGS.both.notes: Lung weight changes may indicate edema, inflammation, or fibrosis. Residual blood can confound. NHP: perfusion/inflation variability makes quantitative screening unreliable.

source_refs.citation: Amato DV et al. 2022. NHP pancreas Tier C qualitative (colony CV ~27-30%).

OM.PANCREAS.both.notes: High variability organ (colony CV ~27-30% for NHP). Not always weighed in NHP tox studies. NHP: qualitative only.

source_refs.citation: FDA Guidance for Industry: Drug-Induced Liver Injury -- Premarketing Clinical Evaluation (2009).
CIOMS Working Group. Drug-Induced Liver Injury (DILI) (2020).
Hall AJ et al. 2012. Liver hypertrophy review (ESTP Expert Workshop). Toxicol Pathol 40(7):971-994.

LB.ALT.up.notes: adverse_floor: 3.0x is FDA 2009 DILI Guidance Grade 3 monitoring threshold for isolated hepatocellular injury, NOT Hy's Law. Hy's Law is a JOINT rule (ALT >= 3x ULN AND TBILI >= 2x ULN AND no alternative explanation) and is deferred to a future joint_rules cycle -- see RG-FCT-LB-BW-08. Downstream reviewers at AC-F4-7 sign-off MUST NOT interpret an isolated ALT finding with verdict: adverse as 'Hy's Law positive' -- Hy's Law requires the joint condition. NHP bands carry a ketamine-anesthesia confound -- blood collected 5-15 min post-injection shows transient elevation; control-matched sampling timing mitigates. NHP band reliability is extrapolated from dog pending a published NHP ALT CV study (RG-FCT-LB-BW-01).

source_refs.citation: FDA 2009 DILI Guidance -- Hy's Law co-criterion.
Hall AJ et al. 2012. Liver hypertrophy review (ESTP). Toxicol Pathol 40(7):971-994.

LB.AST.up.notes: AST is less liver-specific than ALT -- also elevated by muscle (skeletal + cardiac) and RBC injury. AST muscle-origin is NOT distinguishable without CK (creatine kinase) -- see RG-FCT-LB-BW-11. Reviewers at sign-off cross-check CK for AST-isolated findings (AST-up with ALT normal); AST-up + CK-up + ALT normal suggests muscle/cardiac origin rather than hepatic. Paired AST + ALT elevation is the hepatic signal. Ketamine confound applies to NHP per ALT entry.

source_refs.citation: FDA 2009 DILI Guidance -- Hy's Law bilirubin co-criterion (2x ULN).

LB.TBILI.up.notes: adverse_floor: 2.0x is FDA 2009 DILI adverse threshold for isolated direct hyperbilirubinemia, NOT Hy's Law. See RG-FCT-LB-BW-08 for joint-rule deferral. Paired ALT >= 3x + TBILI >= 2x is Hy's Law (the L03 syndrome rule in the registry); univariate TBILI elevation alone is less specific.

source_refs.citation: Hall AJ et al. 2012. ESTP Expert Workshop -- cholestasis criteria.

LB.ALP.up.notes: Dog bands intentionally NOT widened for C-ALP isoenzyme (R1 Finding 3 correction -- research doc internal inconsistency between sec 2.1 text and sec 7.1 JSON resolved in favor of sec 2.1 uniform 1.5/2.0/3.0/5.0). When dog ALP-up co-occurs with TBILI normal AND GGT normal, the finding is likely steroid hepatopathy / enzyme induction, NOT cholestasis -- joint-rule discrimination deferred to RG-FCT-LB-BW-09. Reviewers at sign-off cross-reference LB.TBILI.up and LB.GGT.up verdicts for C-ALP rule-out before treating dog ALP-up as cholestatic. Transport stress / corticosteroids can induce C-ALP isoenzyme elevation > 5x without pathology in dogs (can reach > 100x).

source_refs.citation: Hall AJ et al. 2012. ESTP -- cholestasis / bile duct injury.

LB.GGT.up.notes: Supporting cholestasis marker. Dog GGT higher sensitivity than rodent. NHP extrapolated from dog, ketamine timing confound applies.

source_refs.citation: Published tox-pathology consensus -- BUN >= 1.5-2.0x baseline warrants renal investigation.

LB.BUN.up.notes: BUN is non-specific for renal function -- 2x BUN is often prerenal (dehydration, protein catabolism, GI bleed) rather than renal tubular/glomerular injury. Isolated BUN-up with normal CREAT and concurrent Na/Cl/HCT deviation suggests prerenal rule-in. Joint-rule discrimination (BUN-up without CREAT-up -> downgrade to variation) deferred -- see RG-FCT-LB-BW-18. Paired with CREAT for renal evaluation; prerenal (dehydration) must be ruled out via hematocrit / electrolyte context.

source_refs.citation: Published tox-pathology consensus -- BUN/CREAT paired renal evaluation; CREAT >= 1.5-2.0x baseline warrants renal investigation.

LB.CREAT.up.notes: CREAT is more specific for glomerular filtration than BUN. Prerenal (dehydration) must be ruled out via hematocrit / electrolyte context. Dog and NHP have higher sensitivity than rodent. Paired with BUN for renal evaluation (AC-F1-6 completion -- mirrors LB.BUN.up source family).

source_refs.citation: Published tox-pathology consensus -- enzyme induction / metabolic stress signal.

LB.CHOL.up.notes: Hypercholesterolemia as enzyme induction / metabolic stress signal. NHP is NHP-specific biology (diet-sensitive, stress-sensitive); reliability low.

source_refs.citation: Published tox-pathology consensus -- severe hepatic synthetic failure signal.

LB.CHOL.down.notes: Severe hepatic synthetic failure signal; 50% decrease = adverse, 70% = strong_adverse. fold values are ratio vs baseline: 0.50 means treatment is 50% of control.

source_refs.citation: Published tox-pathology consensus -- hyperglycemia / diabetogenic signal.

LB.GLUC.up.notes: Hyperglycemia / diabetogenic signal. Fasting state critical for interpretation -- non-fasted samples show post-prandial variability masking drug effects. NHP extrapolated; stress/diet effects raise baseline variability.

source_refs.citation: Published tox-pathology consensus -- hypoglycemia / insulin excess / severe hepatic failure signal.

LB.GLUC.down.notes: Hypoglycemia -- may reflect insulin excess, severe hepatic failure, or sampling artifact (delayed glycolysis in unseparated plasma). Sampling artifact must be ruled out before adverse classification.

source_refs.citation: Published tox-pathology consensus -- hepatic synthetic reserve / protein-loss indicators.

LB.TP.down.notes: Globulin vs albumin decomposition NOT distinguishable from TP alone -- see RG-FCT-LB-BW-14 for globulin/A:G ratio. Concurrent LB.ALB.down suggests protein-loss (enteropathy) or hepatic synthetic failure; isolated LB.TP.down with normal ALB suggests globulin loss / immunodeficiency / protein-loss enteropathy-selective. 15% decrease = adverse, 25% = strong_adverse.

source_refs.citation: Published tox-pathology consensus -- hepatic synthetic reserve; ALB has half-life ~14-20 days.

LB.ALB.down.notes: ALB is a sensitive but slow marker of hepatic synthetic reserve (half-life 14-20 days in most species). 15% decrease = adverse (clinically meaningful), 25% = strong_adverse (severe). Often concurrent with LB.TP.down; isolated LB.ALB.down with normal TP suggests selective albumin loss (nephrotic syndrome / protein-loss enteropathy).

rat.notes: Rat/mouse bands extrapolated from Bourges-Abella 2015 dog CVs (research sec 3.3). Rodent within-subject CV is typically higher than dog (~1.5-2x for WBC/EOS/LYM; RBC CV differences smaller). Rodent bands may systematically over-call variation/concern tiers for rodent-typical variability. Honest-uncertainty caveat: provenance: best_practice reflects the extrapolation, not independent rodent-specific empirical calibration -- see RG-FCT-LB-BW-20.

mouse.notes: Mouse band extrapolated from Bourges-Abella 2015 dog CVs (research sec 3.3); rodent within-subject CV typically higher than dog (~1.5-2x for WBC/EOS/LYM; RBC CV differences smaller); bands may systematically over-call variation/concern tiers for rodent-typical variability. Honest-uncertainty caveat: provenance: best_practice reflects the extrapolation, not independent rodent-specific empirical calibration -- see RG-FCT-LB-BW-20.

nhp.notes: NHP dog-extrapolated; ketamine-anesthesia 5-15 min post-injection sampling artifact (transient WBC/LYM shift, less impact on RBC/HGB/HCT/PLT) + n=3-5 power floor.

source_refs.citation: Bourges-Abella N et al. 2015. Biological variation of hematological variables in healthy Beagle dogs, n=55 (Grade B). Vet Clin Pathol.

LB.WBC.up.notes: Inflammation / stress leukogram signal. Stress-leukogram vs immunotox discrimination: pair with LB.EOS.down (stress) or isolated WBC-up with left shift (infection). Stress-leukogram as a joint_rule deferred -- see RG-FCT-LB-BW-08 (sibling of Hy's Law deferral). Reviewers cross-check EOS, LYM, NEUT patterns at sign-off.

source_refs.citation: Bourges-Abella N et al. 2015. Dog hematology biological variation, n=55 Beagles.

LB.WBC.down.notes: Bone-marrow suppression signal; 50% decrease = adverse, 70% = strong_adverse (severe leukopenia with infection risk).

LB.RBC.down.notes: Anemia signal; 15% decrease = adverse. Proportional to HGB and HCT (three-way correlated). Morphology classification (microcytic / macrocytic / hypochromic) requires MCV/MCH/MCHC bands -- RG-FCT-LB-BW-10.

LB.HCT.down.notes: Hematocrit; proportional to RBC. Dehydration can elevate HCT so HCT-down is a pure dilutional-or-marrow-loss signal; HCT-up direction (pre-analytical confound) is not captured here by design.

LB.PLT.down.notes: Thrombocytopenia signal; 50% decrease = adverse, 75% = strong_adverse. PLT variability is the highest of the standard hematology panel (wider variation_ceiling 0.80). DIC detection requires fibrinogen + PT/APTT -- see RG-FCT-LB-BW-15 for coagulation panel.

LB.RETIC.up.notes: Reticulocyte elevation = regenerative response (hemolytic anemia or blood loss). Paired with RBC-down to discriminate regenerative from non-regenerative anemia.

Rodent within-subject CV is typically higher than dog (~1.5-2x for WBC/EOS/LYM; RBC CV differences smaller). LB.RETIC.down.notes: Reticulocyte depression = myelosuppression / non-regenerative response. Paired with RBC-down: RETIC-down + RBC-down = aplastic / non-regenerative anemia (more severe).

LB.LYM.up.notes: Antigenic stimulation signal. NHP CD4:CD8 inversion detection (drug-induced immunotox) requires immunophenotyping (CD4/CD8/CD20/CD16) not captured in univariate LYM -- out of FCT scope, belongs to future immunotoxicity subsystem.

source_refs.citation: Bourges-Abella N et al. 2015.

LB.LYM.down.notes: Immunotox / stress / corticosteroid signal. Stress-leukogram pattern (WBC-up + NEUT-up + LYM-down + EOS-down) documented via cross-endpoint pattern; stress-leukogram as a joint_rule deferred -- RG-FCT-LB-BW-08.

nhp.notes: NHP band reliability at the band level is 'low' / stopping_criterion_used_as_proxy -- 6%/week is a safety cutoff, not an anchor-based MID. STP Sellers 2007 proposal; future anchor-based elicitation (parent cycle F10 P5) may refine. Group-housing social-hierarchy effects and small group size (n=3-5) reduce power.

source_refs.citation: OECD TG 407 (28-day) / TG 408 (90-day oral toxicity -- rodent).
Chapman K et al. 2013. Multi-company BW-loss threshold survey.
Choi T et al. 2011. Beagle historical control CVs (BW).
STP Sellers 2007. NHP 6%/week stopping criterion proposal.

BW.BW.down.notes: NHP stopping criterion = 6% loss per week per STP Sellers 2007; rat/dog/mouse/other stopping criteria (20/20/15/20% respectively) are oversight-policy cutoffs, NOT classifier thresholds -- documented here, not consumed by _verdict_from_bands (no schema version bump per F3-3). Regulatory consensus: >= 10% sustained BW loss adverse in rat / mouse / dog (OECD TG 407/408, Chapman 2013 multi-company). Bands apply to gain inhibition (absence of expected gain) as well as absolute loss in juvenile animals (rats <4 wk, mice <6 wk, NHP age 2-4 y still growing). Dog: 10% loss sustained over 2-3 weeks is the regulatory threshold; pipeline should check duration, not point-in-time (the 4-band ladder does not encode duration -- reviewer sign-off weighs single-timepoint vs sustained). Entry-level threshold_reliability: 'moderate' (not 'high') per M5 honest-uncertainty default (weakest species band governs) -- rat/mouse/dog are regulatory-anchored, NHP is stopping-criterion-proxied; the entry-level tier reflects the NHP band.

rat.notes: Rat/mouse bands extrapolated from Bourges-Abella 2015 dog CVs (research sec 3.3). Rodent within-subject CV is typically higher than dog (~1.5-2x for WBC/EOS/LYM; RBC CV differences smaller). Rodent bands may systematically over-call variation/concern tiers for rodent-typical variability. Honest-uncertainty caveat: provenance: best_practice reflects the extrapolation, not independent rodent-specific empirical calibration -- see RG-FCT-LB-BW-20.

mouse.notes: Mouse band extrapolated from Bourges-Abella 2015 dog CVs (research sec 3.3); rodent within-subject CV typically higher than dog (~1.5-2x for WBC/EOS/LYM; RBC CV differences smaller); bands may systematically over-call variation/concern tiers for rodent-typical variability. Honest-uncertainty caveat: provenance: best_practice reflects the extrapolation, not independent rodent-specific empirical calibration -- see RG-FCT-LB-BW-20.

nhp.notes: NHP dog-extrapolated; ketamine-anesthesia 5-15 min post-injection sampling artifact (transient WBC/LYM shift, less impact on RBC/HGB/HCT/PLT) + n=3-5 power floor.

source_refs.citation: Bourges-Abella N et al. 2015. Dog EOS sensitivity reflected in looser variation_ceiling 0.50 (eosinophil counts fluctuate more than lymphocytes or neutrophils in dog baseline).

LB.EOS.down.notes: Dog-specific stress-leukogram discriminator -- paired with WBC-up + NEUT-up + LYM-down, confirms stress response rather than immunotox. Isolated EOS-down without companion stress-leukogram pattern is less specific. Stress-leukogram joint_rule deferred -- RG-FCT-LB-BW-08.

hys_law._comment: Hy's Law: concurrent ALT + TBILI elevation is a strong signal of DILI. Maps to the L03 rule in shared/rules/lab-clinical-rules.json; the FCT registry carries the regulatory provenance + source refs, the LB rule engine evaluates the per-animal condition.

source_refs.citation: FDA Guidance for Industry: Drug-Induced Liver Injury -- Premarketing Clinical Evaluation (2009).
CIOMS Working Group. Drug-Induced Liver Injury (DILI) (2020).
